dialogue.t: —Gut. Sollen wir ihn nächste Woche mal anrufen, alle zusammen?
dialogue.en: Good. Should we call him next week, all together?
dialogue.lit: Good. Shall we him next week once call, all together?

dialogue.t: —Ja, super Idee! Das würde ihm bestimmt total gefallen.
dialogue.en: Yes, great idea! He'd definitely really like that.
dialogue.lit: Yes, super idea! That would to-him certainly totally please.

dialogue.t: —Genau. Manche Freundschaften halten halt auch über die Entfernung.
dialogue.en: Exactly. Some friendships just hold even across the distance.
dialogue.lit: Exactly. Some friendships hold just-well also over the distance.

dialogue.t: —Hallo? Lukas, hörst du uns? Bild ist gerade eingefroren.
dialogue.en: Hello? Lukas, can you hear us? The picture just froze.
dialogue.lit: Hello? Lukas, hear you us? Picture is right-now frozen.

dialogue.t: —Jetzt geht's wieder! Die Verbindung war kurz schlecht.
dialogue.en: It's working again now! The connection was briefly bad.
dialogue.lit: Now goes-it again! The connection was briefly bad.

dialogue.t: —Endlich seh ich dich auf dem Bildschirm! Wie läuft's denn so?
dialogue.en: Finally I see you on screen! So how's it going?
dialogue.lit: Finally see I you on the screen! How runs-it then so?

dialogue.t: —Echt gut, eigentlich. Der neue Job macht total Spaß.
dialogue.en: Really good, actually. The new job is totally fun.
dialogue.lit: Really good, actually. The new job makes totally fun.

dialogue.t: —Cool, freut mich total! Und hast du schon neue Leute kennengelernt?
dialogue.en: Cool, that makes me really happy! And have you met new people yet?
dialogue.lit: Cool, pleases me totally! And have you already new people met?

dialogue.t: —Ein bisschen, ja. Aber ich verpasse euch trotzdem jeden Tag.
dialogue.en: A bit, yes. But I still miss you guys every day.
dialogue.lit: A little, yes. But I miss you-all nevertheless every day.

dialogue.t: —Wir dich auch! Es ist echt gut, dich mal wieder zu hören.
dialogue.en: Us too! It's really good to hear you again.
dialogue.lit: We you also! It is really good, you once again to hear.

dialogue.t: —Genauso geht's mir. Sagt mal, wie geht's eigentlich Sophie im neuen Job?
dialogue.en: Same here. Hey, how's Sophie actually doing at the new job?
dialogue.lit: Just-so goes-it to-me. Say once, how goes-it actually Sophie in-the new job?

dialogue.t: —Super gut! Sie ist gerade hier, willst du mal direkt mit ihr reden?
dialogue.en: Really well! She's right here, want to talk to her directly?
dialogue.lit: Super good! She is right-now here, want you once directly with her talk?

dialogue.t: —Naja, wenn ich drüber nachdenke, hat sich echt viel verändert.
dialogue.en: Well, when I think about it, a lot has really changed.
dialogue.lit: Well, when I about-it after-think, has itself really much changed.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt total. Sophie ist befördert, Lukas wohnt jetzt weit weg.
dialogue.en: Totally true. Sophie's been promoted, Lukas lives far away now.
dialogue.lit: True totally. Sophie is promoted, Lukas lives now far away.

dialogue.t: —Und Anna und ich haben uns endlich richtig ausgesprochen, übrigens.
dialogue.en: And Anna and I finally really talked it out, by the way.
dialogue.lit: And Anna and I have us finally properly spoken-out, by-the-way.

dialogue.t: —Schön. Trotzdem bleibt's ja irgendwie gleich, oder? Wir helfen uns weiter.
dialogue.en: Nice. Still, it somehow stays the same, right? We still help each other.
dialogue.lit: Nice. Nevertheless stays-it indeed somehow same, or? We help us further.

dialogue.t: —Genau das. Egal wie weit weg jemand wohnt, wir bleiben Freunde.
dialogue.en: Exactly that. No matter how far away someone lives, we stay friends.
dialogue.lit: Exactly that. Equal how far away someone lives, we stay friends.

dialogue.t: —Lukas hat uns ja heute angerufen, hat sich total gut angefühlt.
dialogue.en: Lukas called us today after all, that felt really good.
dialogue.lit: Lukas has us indeed today called, has itself totally good felt.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt, die Verbindung war zwar schlecht am Anfang, aber egal.
dialogue.en: True, the connection was bad at the start, but whatever.
dialogue.lit: True, the connection was admittedly bad at-the start, but equal.

dialogue.t: —Total egal, wir haben uns trotzdem gesehen. Das zählt.
dialogue.en: Totally doesn't matter, we saw each other anyway. That counts.
dialogue.lit: Totally equal, we have us nevertheless seen. That counts.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt eigentlich. Es kommt halt drauf an, wer einem wichtig ist.
dialogue.en: True actually. It really comes down to who matters to you.
dialogue.lit: True actually. It comes just-well to-it on, who to-one important is.

dialogue.t: —Leute, ich hab eine riesige Neuigkeit! Ratet mal.
dialogue.en: Guys, I have huge news! Guess what.
dialogue.lit: People, I have a huge piece-of-news! Guess once.

dialogue.t: —Keine Ahnung, sag schon! Du grinst ja von einem Ohr zum anderen.
dialogue.en: No idea, just say it! You're grinning from ear to ear after all.
dialogue.lit: No idea, say already! You grin indeed from one ear to-the other.

dialogue.t: —Ich bin verlobt! Schaut mal, den Ring hab ich gestern bekommen.
dialogue.en: I'm engaged! Look, I got the ring yesterday.
dialogue.lit: I am engaged! Look once, the ring have I yesterday gotten.

dialogue.t: —Was?! Das kann ich kaum glauben! Herzlichen Glückwunsch, Tom!
dialogue.en: What?! I can hardly believe it! Congratulations, Tom!
dialogue.lit: What?! That can I barely believe! Heartfelt congratulation, Tom!

dialogue.t: —Danke! Sie hat ja gesagt, ich konnte es selbst kaum fassen.
dialogue.en: Thanks! She said yes, I could barely take it in myself.
dialogue.lit: Thanks! She has indeed yes said, I could it myself barely grasp.

dialogue.t: —Komm her, ich muss dich umarmen! Das ist riesig.
dialogue.en: Come here, I have to hug you! This is huge.
dialogue.lit: Come here, I must you embrace! That is huge.

dialogue.t: —Danke euch allen. Ich hätte das nie erwartet, ehrlich gesagt.
dialogue.en: Thanks, everyone. I never would have expected that, honestly.
dialogue.lit: Thanks to-you-all. I would-have that never expected, honestly said.

dialogue.t: —Müssen wir das nicht sofort feiern? Das ist eine riesige Sache!
dialogue.en: Don't we have to celebrate this right away? This is a huge deal!
dialogue.lit: Must we that not immediately celebrate? That is a huge thing!

dialogue.t: —Auf jeden Fall! Aber zuerst muss ich auch Lukas anrufen.
dialogue.en: Definitely! But first I have to call Lukas too.
dialogue.lit: On every case! But first must I also Lukas call.

dialogue.t: —Tom, wo ist eigentlich deine Verlobte? Ich will sie endlich kennenlernen.
dialogue.en: Tom, where's your fiancée actually? I'd love to finally meet her.
dialogue.lit: Tom, where is actually your engaged-one? I want her finally to-meet.

dialogue.t: —Sie kommt gleich! Sie musste noch kurz was holen.
dialogue.en: She's coming soon! She had to quickly grab something.
dialogue.lit: She comes right-away! She had-to still briefly something fetch.

dialogue.t: —Ah, da ist sie ja! Hallo, freut mich riesig, dich kennenzulernen!
dialogue.en: Ah, there she is! Hi, so happy to meet you!
dialogue.lit: Ah, there is she indeed! Hi, pleases me hugely, you to-meet!

dialogue.t: —Hallo! Tom hat so viel von euch erzählt. Endlich treffen wir uns.
dialogue.en: Hi! Tom has told me so much about you. We finally meet.
dialogue.lit: Hi! Tom has so much from you-all told. Finally meet we us.

dialogue.t: —Wann habt ihr euch eigentlich verlobt? Erzähl die ganze Geschichte!
dialogue.en: When did you guys actually get engaged? Tell the whole story!
dialogue.lit: When have you-all yourselves actually engaged? Tell the whole story!

dialogue.t: —Letzte Woche, beim Sonnenuntergang im Park. Total romantisch.
dialogue.en: Last week, at sunset in the park. Totally romantic.
dialogue.lit: Last week, at-the sunset in-the park. Totally romantic.

dialogue.t: —So schön! Lasst uns auf die Braut und den Bräutigam anstoßen.
dialogue.en: So lovely! Let's toast to the bride and groom.
dialogue.lit: So beautiful! Let us to the bride and the groom toast.

dialogue.t: —*Alle stoßen an* — Auf Tom und seine Zukunft, herzlichen Glückwunsch!
dialogue.en: *Everyone clinks glasses* — To Tom and his future, congratulations!
dialogue.lit: *All clink-on* — To Tom and his future, heartfelt congratulation!

dialogue.t: —Danke, das bedeutet mir so viel, dass ihr alle hier seid.
dialogue.en: Thanks, it means so much to me that you're all here.
dialogue.lit: Thanks, that means to-me so much, that you-all all here are.

lessons.titleTgt: Was für ein Jahr, schon wieder

dialogue.t: —Wenn ich zurückdenke, ist echt unglaublich viel passiert dieses Jahr.
dialogue.en: When I think back, an incredible amount has really happened this year.
dialogue.lit: When I back-think, is really unbelievably much happened this year.

dialogue.t: —Total. Lukas ist umgezogen, Sophie wurde befördert, Tom ist verlobt.
dialogue.en: Totally. Lukas moved, Sophie got promoted, Tom's engaged.
dialogue.lit: Totally. Lukas is moved, Sophie became promoted, Tom is engaged.

dialogue.t: —Und ich hab Lena nach zehn Jahren wiedergetroffen, weißt du noch?
dialogue.en: And I met Lena again after ten years, remember?
dialogue.lit: And I have Lena after ten years again-met, know you still?

dialogue.t: —Klar! Ihr habt euch ja kaum erkannt, am Anfang.
dialogue.en: Sure! You guys barely recognized each other, at the start.
dialogue.lit: Clear! You-all have yourselves indeed barely recognized, at-the start.

dialogue.t: —Genau, total verändert hat sie sich nicht, aber irgendwie schon auch.
dialogue.en: Exactly, she hasn't totally changed, but somehow also yes.
dialogue.lit: Exactly, totally changed has she herself not, but somehow indeed also.

dialogue.t: —Verstehe ich. Und wie geht's eigentlich mit Anna und dir jetzt?
dialogue.en: I understand. And how are things with you and Anna now, actually?
dialogue.lit: Understand I. And how goes-it actually with Anna and you now?

dialogue.t: —Sehr gut, ehrlich. Wir haben uns ja ausgesprochen, alles ist gut.
dialogue.en: Very good, honestly. We talked it out after all, everything's fine.
dialogue.lit: Very good, honestly. We have us indeed spoken-out, everything is good.

dialogue.t: —Schön zu hören. Diese Freundesgruppe hält wirklich alles aus, oder?
dialogue.en: Nice to hear. This friend group really weathers everything, doesn't it?
dialogue.lit: Nice to hear. This friend-group holds really everything out, or?

dialogue.t: —Stimmt total. Auf noch ein verrücktes, gutes Jahr!
dialogue.en: Totally true. Here's to another crazy, good year!
dialogue.lit: True totally. To still a crazy, good year!

dialogue.t: —Hey, du trainierst ja jetzt allein hier? Ohne Lukas, mein ich.
dialogue.en: Hey, so you're training alone here now? Without Lukas, I mean.
dialogue.lit: Hey, you train indeed now alone here? Without Lukas, mean I.

dialogue.t: —Ja, ist schon komisch, ehrlich gesagt. Aber ich bin halt drangeblieben.
dialogue.en: Yes, it's a bit weird, honestly. But I just kept at it.
dialogue.lit: Yes, is already strange, honestly said. But I am just-well stuck-with-it.

dialogue.t: —Respekt! Hast du eine feste Routine jetzt?
dialogue.en: Respect! Do you have a fixed routine now?
dialogue.lit: Respect! Have you a fixed routine now?

dialogue.t: —Ja, eigentlich schon. Montag, Mittwoch, Freitag, immer um sechs.
dialogue.en: Yes, actually yes. Monday, Wednesday, Friday, always at six.
dialogue.lit: Yes, actually indeed. Monday, Wednesday, Friday, always at six.

dialogue.t: —Cool, das motiviert mich total. Vielleicht sollt ich auch wieder anfangen.
dialogue.en: Cool, that's really motivating me. Maybe I should also start again.
dialogue.lit: Cool, that motivates me totally. Maybe should I also again start.

dialogue.t: —Mach das! Wir könnten zusammen trainieren, wenn du magst.
dialogue.en: Do it! We could train together if you like.
dialogue.lit: Make that! We could together train, if you like.

dialogue.t: —Ernsthaft? Das wär super, dann hab ich auch jemand, der mich motiviert.
dialogue.en: Seriously? That would be great, then I'd also have someone who motivates me.
dialogue.lit: Seriously? That would-be great, then have I also someone, who me motivates.

dialogue.t: —Genau, gegenseitig halt. Treffen wir uns morgen früh hier?
dialogue.en: Exactly, mutually. Shall we meet here tomorrow morning?
dialogue.lit: Exactly, mutually just-well. Meet we us tomorrow early here?

dialogue.t: —Klar, abgemacht! Lukas wär bestimmt stolz auf uns beide.
dialogue.en: Sure, deal! Lukas would surely be proud of both of us.
dialogue.lit: Clear, agreed! Lukas would-be certainly proud on us both.

dialogue.t: —Sophie, ich muss dir was erzählen. Ich hab einen verrückten Plan.
dialogue.en: Sophie, I have to tell you something. I have a crazy plan.
dialogue.lit: Sophie, I must to-you something tell. I have a crazy plan.

dialogue.t: —Erzähl! Du klingst total aufgeregt.
dialogue.en: Tell me! You sound totally excited.
dialogue.lit: Tell! You sound totally excited.

dialogue.t: —Ich überlege, ein eigenes kleines Geschäft zu eröffnen. Vielleicht einen Laden für frisches Obst.
dialogue.en: I'm thinking about opening my own little shop. Maybe a fresh fruit store.
dialogue.lit: I consider, an own small shop to open. Maybe a shop for fresh fruit.

dialogue.t: —Was?! Anna, das ist ja dein Traum seit Jahren!
dialogue.en: What?! Anna, that's been your dream for years!
dialogue.lit: What?! Anna, that is indeed your dream since years!

dialogue.t: —Genau, und ich denke, jetzt wäre vielleicht der richtige Moment, das umzusetzen.
dialogue.en: Exactly, and I think now might be the right moment to make it happen.
dialogue.lit: Exactly, and I think, now would-be maybe the right moment, that to-implement.

dialogue.t: —Das musst du unbedingt machen! Ich helf dir gern, wo ich kann.
dialogue.en: You absolutely have to do it! I'll gladly help wherever I can.
dialogue.lit: That must you absolutely make! I help you gladly, where I can.

dialogue.t: —Wirklich? Das würde mir so viel bedeuten, ehrlich.
dialogue.en: Really? That would mean so much to me, honestly.
dialogue.lit: Really? That would to-me so much mean, honestly.

dialogue.t: —Klar! Vielleicht kann uns Lukas sogar von dort Tipps geben, er kennt sich mit sowas aus.
dialogue.en: Sure! Maybe Lukas can even give us tips from there, he knows about that kind of thing.
dialogue.lit: Clear! Maybe can us Lukas even from there tips give, he knows himself with such-thing out.

dialogue.t: —Gute Idee. So, jetzt fängt's also wirklich an. Mein eigener Laden.
dialogue.en: Good idea. So, it really begins now then. My own shop.
dialogue.lit: Good idea. So, now begins-it so really on. My own shop.

dialogue.t: —Wow, der Laden sieht schon richtig gut aus! Wie läuft die Renovierung?
dialogue.en: Wow, the shop already looks really good! How's the renovation going?
dialogue.lit: Wow, the shop looks already really good out! How runs the renovation?

dialogue.t: —Echt gut, eigentlich. Wir sind fast fertig, nur noch das Schild fehlt.
dialogue.en: Really good, actually. We're almost done, just the sign is still missing.
dialogue.lit: Really good, actually. We are almost finished, only still the sign is-missing.

dialogue.t: —Wann genau soll die Eröffnung sein? Hast du schon ein Datum?
dialogue.en: When exactly is the opening supposed to be? Do you have a date yet?
dialogue.lit: When exactly shall the opening be? Have you already a date?

dialogue.t: —Nächsten Samstag, hoffentlich. Wenn alles rechtzeitig fertig wird.
dialogue.en: Next Saturday, hopefully. If everything's finished in time.
dialogue.lit: Next Saturday, hopefully. If everything in-time finished becomes.

dialogue.t: —Und die Lieferanten? Hast du schon mit denen gesprochen, wegen dem Obst?
dialogue.en: And the suppliers? Have you already talked to them about the fruit?
dialogue.lit: And the suppliers? Have you already with them spoken, because-of the fruit?

dialogue.t: —Ja, alles geklärt. Sie liefern jeden Morgen frisches Obst und Gemüse.
dialogue.en: Yes, all sorted. They deliver fresh fruit and vegetables every morning.
dialogue.lit: Yes, everything clarified. They deliver every morning fresh fruit and vegetable.

dialogue.t: —Perfekt. Bist du bereit für die Eröffnung, würdest du sagen?
dialogue.en: Perfect. Are you ready for the opening, would you say?
dialogue.lit: Perfect. Are you ready for the opening, would you say?

dialogue.t: —Naja, ein bisschen nervös bin ich schon. Aber auch total aufgeregt.
dialogue.en: Well, I am a little nervous. But also totally excited.
dialogue.lit: Well, a little nervous am I already. But also totally excited.

dialogue.t: —Das ist normal! Wir sind alle am Samstag da, versprochen.
dialogue.en: That's normal! We'll all be there on Saturday, promise.
dialogue.lit: That is normal! We are all on-the Saturday there, promised.

dialogue.t: —Anna, schau mal aus dem Fenster, da steht schon eine Schlange!
dialogue.en: Anna, look out the window, there's already a line!
dialogue.lit: Anna, look once out the window, there stands already a line!

dialogue.t: —Was?! Schon so früh? Ich kann's kaum glauben!
dialogue.en: What?! Already this early? I can hardly believe it!
dialogue.lit: What?! Already so early? I can-it barely believe!

dialogue.t: —Komm, du musst das Band durchschneiden, alle warten auf dich.
dialogue.en: Come on, you have to cut the ribbon, everyone's waiting for you.
dialogue.lit: Come, you must the ribbon cut-through, all wait on you.

dialogue.t: —Okay, ich bin bereit. *Sie durchschneidet das Band* — Mein Laden ist offiziell offen!
dialogue.en: Okay, I'm ready. *She cuts the ribbon* — My shop is officially open!
dialogue.lit: Okay, I am ready. *She cuts-through the ribbon* — My shop is officially open!

dialogue.t: —Herzlichen Glückwunsch! Schau dir die ganzen Kunden an, das ist riesig!
dialogue.en: Congratulations! Look at all these customers, this is huge!
dialogue.lit: Heartfelt congratulation! Look you-to-yourself the whole customers, that is huge!

dialogue.t: —Ich bin sprachlos. So viele Leute, ich hätte nie damit gerechnet.
dialogue.en: I'm speechless. So many people, I never would have expected this.
dialogue.lit: I am speechless. So many people, I would-have never with-it reckoned.

dialogue.t: —Das ist dein Erfolg, Anna. Du hast dafür so hart gearbeitet.
dialogue.en: This is your success, Anna. You worked so hard for this.
dialogue.lit: That is your success, Anna. You have for-it so hard worked.

dialogue.t: —Danke euch allen. Ohne eure Hilfe wäre das nie passiert.
dialogue.en: Thanks, everyone. Without your help this never would have happened.
dialogue.lit: Thanks to-you-all. Without your help would-be that never happened.

dialogue.t: —Wir sind alle so stolz auf dich. Jetzt verkauf endlich Äpfel, die Kunden warten!
dialogue.en: We're all so proud of you. Now go sell some apples already, the customers are waiting!
dialogue.lit: We are all so proud on you. Now sell finally apples, the customers wait!

lessons.titleTgt: Eine Bilanz nach so viel Zeit
lessons.titleEn: Taking stock after so much time

dialogue.t: —Wenn ich so darüber nachdenke, hat sich wirklich alles entwickelt, oder?
dialogue.en: When I think about it like this, everything's really developed, hasn't it?
dialogue.lit: When I so about-it after-think, has itself really everything developed, or?

dialogue.t: —Total. Anna hat ihren eigenen Laden, Tom ist verlobt, Sophie ist befördert.
dialogue.en: Totally. Anna has her own shop, Tom's engaged, Sophie's promoted.
dialogue.lit: Totally. Anna has her own shop, Tom is engaged, Sophie is promoted.

dialogue.t: —Und Lukas, auch wenn er weit weg wohnt, ist immer noch Teil von uns.
dialogue.en: And Lukas, even though he lives far away, is still part of us.
dialogue.lit: And Lukas, even-though he far away lives, is still part of us.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt total. Wir telefonieren ja regelmäßig, fühlt sich gar nicht so weit an.
dialogue.en: Totally true. We call regularly after all, doesn't feel that far at all.
dialogue.lit: True totally. We telephone indeed regularly, feels itself at-all not so far on.

dialogue.t: —Genau. Und ich hab ja auch Lena wieder im Leben, das war auch unerwartet schön.
dialogue.en: Exactly. And I also have Lena back in my life, that was unexpectedly nice too.
dialogue.lit: Exactly. And I have indeed also Lena again in-the life, that was also unexpectedly nice.

dialogue.t: —Krass, wenn man bedenkt, wie viel sich in so kurzer Zeit verändert hat.
dialogue.en: Wild, when you consider how much has changed in such a short time.
dialogue.lit: Wild, when one considers, how much itself in such short time changed has.

dialogue.t: —Wahrscheinlich war's die ganze Zeit schon so, wir haben's nur nicht gemerkt.
dialogue.en: It was probably like this the whole time, we just didn't notice.
dialogue.lit: Probably was-it the whole time already so, we have-it only not noticed.

dialogue.t: —Vielleicht. Aber egal, wie sich alles ändert — wir bleiben zusammen.
dialogue.en: Maybe. But no matter how everything changes — we stay together.
dialogue.lit: Maybe. But equal, how itself everything changes — we stay together.

dialogue.t: —Genau das. Auf uns, und auf alles, was noch kommt.
dialogue.en: Exactly that. To us, and to everything still to come.
dialogue.lit: Exactly that. To us, and to everything, what still comes.

dialogue.t: —Da bist du ja, am Hauptbahnhof! Moin, Lukas!
dialogue.en: There you are, at the main station! Morning, Lukas!
dialogue.lit: There are you indeed, at-the main-station! Morning, Lukas!

dialogue.t: —Moin moin! Schön, dass ihr endlich vorbeischaut!
dialogue.en: Morning, morning! So glad you finally came by!
dialogue.lit: Morning morning! Beautiful, that you-all finally by-look!

dialogue.t: —Wir konnten's kaum erwarten, ehrlich. Wie ist die Stadt hier so?
dialogue.en: We could hardly wait, honestly. So what's the city like here?
dialogue.lit: We could-it barely await, honestly. How is the city here so?

dialogue.t: —Echt nice, muss ich sagen. Ganz anders als bei uns, aber gut.
dialogue.en: Really nice, I have to say. Quite different from back home, but good.
dialogue.lit: Really nice, must I say. Quite different than at us, but good.

dialogue.t: —Krass, hier ist ja alles bisschen ruhiger als bei uns.
dialogue.en: Wild, everything's a bit calmer here than back home.
dialogue.lit: Wild, here is indeed everything a-little calmer than at us.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt, find ich aber gar nicht schlecht. Kommt, ich zeig euch alles!
dialogue.en: True, but I don't think that's bad at all. Come on, I'll show you everything!
dialogue.lit: True, find I but at-all not bad. Come, I show you-all everything!

dialogue.t: —Top! Wir haben extra ein ganzes Wochenende eingeplant, nur für dich.
dialogue.en: Awesome! We specifically planned a whole weekend, just for you.
dialogue.lit: Top! We have specially a whole weekend planned, only for you.

dialogue.t: —Das bedeutet mir richtig viel, ehrlich. Kommt, lasst uns loslegen.
dialogue.en: That really means a lot to me, honestly. Come on, let's get going.
dialogue.lit: That means to-me really much, honestly. Come, let us start.

dialogue.t: —Hier ist der Hafen, mein absoluter Lieblingsplatz hier in der Stadt.
dialogue.en: Here's the harbor, my absolute favorite spot in this city.
dialogue.lit: Here is the harbor, my absolute favorite-place here in the city.

dialogue.t: —Wow, die Aussicht hier ist echt der Hammer!
dialogue.en: Wow, the view here is really incredible!
dialogue.lit: Wow, the view here is really the hammer!

dialogue.t: —Ne, oder? Wollt ihr was Echtes probieren, norddeutsche Spezialität?
dialogue.en: Right? Want to try something authentic, a north-German specialty?
dialogue.lit: No, or? Want you-all something real to-try, north-German specialty?

dialogue.t: —Auf jeden Fall! Was schlägst du vor?
dialogue.en: Definitely! What do you suggest?
dialogue.lit: On every case! What suggest you forward?

dialogue.t: —Lecker Fisch, hier am Hafen, frisch gefangen heute Morgen.
dialogue.en: Tasty fish, here at the harbor, freshly caught this morning.
dialogue.lit: Tasty fish, here at the harbor, freshly caught this morning.

dialogue.t: —Klingt super. Ich hab ja noch nie hier Fisch gegessen.
dialogue.en: Sounds great. I've never actually had fish here.
dialogue.lit: Sounds super. I have indeed never here fish eaten.

dialogue.t: —Dann wird's höchste Zeit! Kommt, der Stand ist nicht weit.
dialogue.en: Then it's high time! Come on, the stand isn't far.
dialogue.lit: Then becomes-it highest time! Come, the stand is not far.

dialogue.t: —Mensch, Lukas, du klingst ja schon richtig norddeutsch.
dialogue.en: Man, Lukas, you're already sounding really north-German.
dialogue.lit: Man, Lukas, you sound indeed already really north-German.

dialogue.t: —Haha, na ja, ein bisschen schon. Man passt sich halt an.
dialogue.en: Haha, well, a bit, yeah. You just adapt.
dialogue.lit: Haha, well yes, a little already. One adapts oneself just-well to.

dialogue.t: —Schau dir die Lichter über dem Wasser an, einfach wunderschön.
dialogue.en: Look at the lights over the water, just beautiful.
dialogue.lit: Look you-to-yourself the lights over the water, simply wonderfully-beautiful.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt. Lass uns kurz hier stehen bleiben und tief durchatmen.
dialogue.en: True. Let's just stand here a bit and breathe deeply.
dialogue.lit: True. Let us briefly here stand stay and deeply through-breathe.

dialogue.t: —Lukas, fühlst du dich hier mittlerweile richtig wohl?
dialogue.en: Lukas, do you feel at home here by now, really?
dialogue.lit: Lukas, feel you yourself here meanwhile really well?

dialogue.t: —Ja, eigentlich schon. Es ist anders als bei euch, aber gut anders.
dialogue.en: Yes, actually yes. It's different from with you guys, but good different.
dialogue.lit: Yes, actually indeed. It is different than at you-all, but good different.

dialogue.t: —Das freut mich total zu hören. Ich hatte schon Sorge, dass du einsam bist.
dialogue.en: That makes me really happy to hear. I was already worried you'd be lonely.
dialogue.lit: That pleases me totally to hear. I had already worry, that you lonely are.

dialogue.t: —Naja, am Anfang schon, ehrlich gesagt. Aber jetzt genieß ich die Ruhe hier.
dialogue.en: Well, at first yes, to be honest. But now I enjoy the calm here.
dialogue.lit: Well, at-the start indeed, honestly said. But now enjoy I the calm here.

dialogue.t: —Verstehe ich gut. Manchmal braucht man genau das.
dialogue.en: I understand that well. Sometimes you need exactly that.
dialogue.lit: Understand I well. Sometimes needs one exactly that.

dialogue.t: —Genau. Und ich weiß ja, ihr seid nur einen Zug entfernt.
dialogue.en: Exactly. And I know you're only a train ride away.
dialogue.lit: Exactly. And I know indeed, you-all are only a train away.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt. Wir bleiben sowieso für immer verbunden, egal wie weit.
dialogue.en: True. We stay connected forever anyway, no matter how far.
dialogue.lit: True. We stay anyway for ever connected, equal how far.

dialogue.t: —Schon wieder Abschied. Das fällt mir echt jedes Mal schwer.
dialogue.en: Goodbye again. This is really hard for me every time.
dialogue.lit: Already again goodbye. That falls to-me really every time hard.

dialogue.t: —Mir auch, ehrlich. Aber diesmal war's ja nur ein Wochenende, kein Umzug.
dialogue.en: Me too, honestly. But this time it was just a weekend, not a move.
dialogue.lit: To-me also, honestly. But this-time was-it indeed only a weekend, not a move.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt schon. Hast du alles in deiner Tasche? Wir müssen gleich los.
dialogue.en: True enough. Do you have everything in your bag? We have to go soon.
dialogue.lit: True already. Have you everything in your bag? We must right-away off.

dialogue.t: —Ja, alles gepackt. War echt ein unglaubliches Wochenende, danke, Lukas.
dialogue.en: Yes, all packed. It was really an incredible weekend, thanks, Lukas.
dialogue.lit: Yes, everything packed. Was really an unbelievable weekend, thanks, Lukas.

dialogue.t: —Ich hab euch zu danken! Das hat mir so viel bedeutet, dass ihr gekommen seid.
dialogue.en: I should be thanking you! It meant so much to me that you came.
dialogue.lit: I have you-all to-thank! That has to-me so much meant, that you-all come are.

dialogue.t: —Auf Wiedersehen, Lukas! Wir sehen uns bald wieder, versprochen.
dialogue.en: Goodbye, Lukas! We'll see each other again soon, promise.
dialogue.lit: On again-seeing, Lukas! We see us soon again, promised.

dialogue.t: —Bis bald! Vielleicht komm ich ja sogar mal zu euch, nächstes Mal.
dialogue.en: See you soon! Maybe I'll even come visit you guys next time.
dialogue.lit: Until soon! Maybe come I indeed even once to you-all, next time.

dialogue.t: —Das wäre so schön! Wir warten drauf. Mach's gut, Lukas!
dialogue.en: That would be so nice! We'll be waiting for it. Take care, Lukas!
dialogue.lit: That would-be so beautiful! We wait on-it. Make-it good, Lukas!

dialogue.t: —Wie war's bei Lukas? Erzähl, ich will alles wissen!
dialogue.en: How was it at Lukas'? Tell me, I want to know everything!
dialogue.lit: How was-it at Lukas? Tell, I want everything know!

dialogue.t: —Echt schön, ehrlich. Die Stadt ist anders, aber gut, und der Hafen ist der Hammer.
dialogue.en: Really nice, honestly. The city is different, but good, and the harbor's incredible.
dialogue.lit: Really beautiful, honestly. The city is different, but good, and the harbor is the hammer.

dialogue.t: —Klingt großartig! Wirkte er glücklich dort?
dialogue.en: Sounds great! Did he seem happy there?
dialogue.lit: Sounds great! Seemed he happy there?

dialogue.t: —Ja, total. Er fühlt sich mittlerweile richtig wohl, sagt er.
dialogue.en: Yes, totally. He says he feels really at home now.
dialogue.lit: Yes, totally. He feels himself meanwhile really well, says he.

dialogue.t: —Schön zu hören. Wir haben ihn echt gefehlt, oder?
dialogue.en: Nice to hear. We really missed him, right?
dialogue.lit: Nice to hear. We have him really missed, or?

dialogue.t: —Total, aber jetzt ist's okay. Wir telefonieren ja sowieso ständig.
dialogue.en: Totally, but now it's okay. We call constantly anyway.
dialogue.lit: Totally, but now is-it okay. We telephone indeed anyway constantly.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt. So, und was steht diese Woche bei dir so an?
dialogue.en: True. So, what's on your plate this week?
dialogue.lit: True. So, and what stands this week at you so on?

dialogue.t: —Viel im Laden, eigentlich. Und Sophie und Tom planen die Hochzeit.
dialogue.en: A lot at the shop, actually. And Sophie and Tom are planning the wedding.
dialogue.lit: Much in-the shop, actually. And Sophie and Tom plan the wedding.

dialogue.t: —Oh, stimmt! Da kommt ja noch eine richtig große Feier auf uns zu.
dialogue.en: Oh, right! There's a really big celebration coming our way.
dialogue.lit: Oh, true! There comes indeed still a really big celebration on us to.

dialogue.t: —So, Sophie, lass uns mal die Hochzeit planen. Habt ihr schon ein Datum?
dialogue.en: So, Sophie, let's plan the wedding. Do you guys have a date yet?
dialogue.lit: So, Sophie, let us once the wedding plan. Have you-all already a date?

dialogue.t: —Wir denken an nächsten Sommer, aber der Termin ist noch nicht ganz fest.
dialogue.en: We're thinking next summer, but the date isn't completely fixed yet.
dialogue.lit: We think on next summer, but the date is still not completely fixed.

dialogue.t: —Cool! Und habt ihr schon einen Saal im Kopf?
dialogue.en: Cool! And do you have a venue in mind already?
dialogue.lit: Cool! And have you-all already a hall in-the head?

dialogue.t: —Ja, einen ganz schönen, nicht weit vom Park. Da, wo Tom mich gefragt hat.
dialogue.en: Yes, a really nice one, not far from the park. The place where Tom proposed to me.
dialogue.lit: Yes, a quite beautiful-one, not far from-the park. There, where Tom me asked has.

dialogue.t: —Wie romantisch! Und wie steht's mit der Gästeliste?
dialogue.en: How romantic! And how's it going with the guest list?
dialogue.lit: How romantic! And how stands-it with the guest-list?

dialogue.t: —Wir laden so um die hundert Leute ein, aber das ändert sich bestimmt noch.
dialogue.en: We're inviting around a hundred people, but that'll probably change still.
dialogue.lit: We invite so around the hundred people in, but that changes itself certainly still.

dialogue.t: —Wahnsinn, hundert Leute! Brauchst du Hilfe bei der Organisation?
dialogue.en: Wild, a hundred people! Do you need help with the organizing?
dialogue.lit: Madness, hundred people! Need you help with the organization?

dialogue.t: —Auf jeden Fall, das wär super! Du bist ja so gut in sowas.
dialogue.en: Definitely, that would be great! You're so good at this kind of thing.
dialogue.lit: On every case, that would-be super! You are indeed so good in such-thing.

dialogue.t: —Dann machen wir das zusammen. Wird die schönste Hochzeit überhaupt.
dialogue.en: Then we'll do it together. It's going to be the most beautiful wedding ever.
dialogue.lit: Then make we that together. Becomes the most-beautiful wedding at-all.

dialogue.t: —Sophie, probier doch das hier mal an! Ich glaub, das könnte perfekt sein.
dialogue.en: Sophie, try this one on! I think this could be perfect.
dialogue.lit: Sophie, try indeed that here once on! I believe, that could perfect be.

dialogue.t: —Okay, einen Moment. *Sie kommt zurück* — Und? Wie steht's mir?
dialogue.en: Okay, one moment. *She comes back* — So? How does it look on me?
dialogue.lit: Okay, a moment. *She comes back* — And? How stands-it to-me?

dialogue.t: —Sophie... du siehst wunderschön aus! Schau dich mal im Spiegel an.
dialogue.en: Sophie... you look beautiful! Take a look at yourself in the mirror.
dialogue.lit: Sophie... you look wonderfully-beautiful out! Look you-to-yourself once in the mirror.

dialogue.t: —Oh mein Gott, das ist es! Das ist genau das Kleid, das ich wollte.
dialogue.en: Oh my god, this is it! This is exactly the dress I wanted.
dialogue.lit: Oh my god, that is it! That is exactly the dress, that I wanted.

dialogue.t: —Du weinst ja schon! Das steht dir wirklich perfekt, Sophie.
dialogue.en: You're already crying! It really suits you perfectly, Sophie.
dialogue.lit: You cry indeed already! That stands to-you really perfectly, Sophie.

dialogue.t: —Ich kann's selbst kaum glauben. Tom wird sprachlos sein, oder?
dialogue.en: I can barely believe it myself. Tom will be speechless, right?
dialogue.lit: I can-it myself barely believe. Tom becomes speechless be, or?

dialogue.t: —Total sprachlos! Das ist DAS Kleid, Sophie. Kein Zweifel.
dialogue.en: Totally speechless! This is THE dress, Sophie. No doubt.
dialogue.lit: Totally speechless! That is THE dress, Sophie. No doubt.

dialogue.t: —Danke, dass du heute mitgekommen bist. Das bedeutet mir alles.
dialogue.en: Thanks for coming along today. That means everything to me.
dialogue.lit: Thanks, that you today along-come are. That means to-me everything.

dialogue.t: —Immer doch! Jetzt nehmen wir das Kleid, und dann feiern wir mit Kaffee.
dialogue.en: Of course! Now let's get the dress, and then we'll celebrate with coffee.
dialogue.lit: Always indeed! Now take we the dress, and then celebrate we with coffee.

lessons.titleEn: Lukas is coming to the wedding

dialogue.t: —Lukas, hast du die Einladung zur Hochzeit bekommen?
dialogue.en: Lukas, did you get the wedding invitation?
dialogue.lit: Lukas, have you the invitation to-the wedding gotten?

dialogue.t: —Ja, hab ich! Ich freu mich riesig, ich komm auf jeden Fall.
dialogue.en: Yes, I got it! I'm super excited, I'm definitely coming.
dialogue.lit: Yes, have I! I rejoice me hugely, I come on every case.

dialogue.t: —Super! Nimmst du den Zug, oder wie kommst du?
dialogue.en: Great! Are you taking the train, or how are you coming?
dialogue.lit: Super! Take you the train, or how come you?

dialogue.t: —Ich nehm den Zug, klar. Ich will rechtzeitig da sein, keine Sorge.
dialogue.en: I'll take the train, sure. I want to be there on time, don't worry.
dialogue.lit: I take the train, clear. I want in-time there be, no worry.

dialogue.t: —Gut. Hast du eigentlich schon einen Anzug für die Feier?
dialogue.en: Good. Do you already have a suit for the celebration, by the way?
dialogue.lit: Good. Have you actually already a suit for the celebration?

dialogue.t: —Naja, noch nicht ganz. Ich muss mir noch einen kaufen, ehrlich gesagt.
dialogue.en: Well, not quite yet. I still have to buy myself one, to be honest.
dialogue.lit: Well, still not quite. I must to-myself still one buy, honestly said.

dialogue.t: —Dann beeil dich mal! Die Hochzeit ist schneller da, als du denkst.
dialogue.en: Then hurry up! The wedding will be here faster than you think.
dialogue.lit: Then hurry yourself once! The wedding is faster there, than you think.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt, mach ich. Ich freu mich so, euch alle wiederzusehen.
dialogue.en: True, I will. I'm so happy to see you all again.
dialogue.lit: True, make I. I rejoice me so, you-all again-to-see.

dialogue.t: —Wir uns auch! Bis dann, Lukas. Bring deinen Anzug mit!
dialogue.en: Us too! See you then, Lukas. Bring your suit!
dialogue.lit: We us also! Until then, Lukas. Bring your suit with!

dialogue.t: —Sophie, wie fühlst du dich? Heute ist endlich der große Tag!
dialogue.en: Sophie, how are you feeling? Today's finally the big day!
dialogue.lit: Sophie, how feel you yourself? Today is finally the big day!

dialogue.t: —Ehrlich gesagt, ein bisschen nervös. Aber auch total glücklich.
dialogue.en: Honestly, a little nervous. But also totally happy.
dialogue.lit: Honestly said, a little nervous. But also totally happy.

dialogue.t: —Das ist normal, ganz ruhig. Die Blumen sind übrigens schon angekommen.
dialogue.en: That's normal, calm down. The flowers have already arrived, by the way.
dialogue.lit: That is normal, quite calmly. The flowers are by-the-way already arrived.

dialogue.t: —Oh, super! Und meine Frisur, hast du jemanden dafür organisiert?
dialogue.en: Oh, great! And my hair, did you organize someone for that?
dialogue.lit: Oh, super! And my hairstyle, have you someone for-it organized?

dialogue.t: —Klar, der Friseur kommt in einer Stunde. Hab alles im Griff, versprochen.
dialogue.en: Sure, the hairdresser comes in an hour. I've got everything under control, promise.
dialogue.lit: Clear, the hairdresser comes in an hour. Have everything in-the grip, promised.

dialogue.t: —Danke, Anna. Ich wüsste echt nicht, was ich ohne dich machen würde.
dialogue.en: Thanks, Anna. I really don't know what I'd do without you.
dialogue.lit: Thanks, Anna. I would-know really not, what I without you make would.

dialogue.t: —Dafür sind Schwestern doch da. Jetzt komm, lass uns dich fertig machen.
dialogue.en: That's what sisters are for, after all. Now come on, let's get you ready.
dialogue.lit: For-that are sisters indeed there. Now come, let us you ready make.

dialogue.t: —Okay. Ist Lukas eigentlich schon angekommen?
dialogue.en: Okay. Has Lukas actually arrived yet?
dialogue.lit: Okay. Is Lukas actually already arrived?

dialogue.t: —Ja, vor einer Stunde schon. Er sieht übrigens richtig gut aus im Anzug!
dialogue.en: Yes, an hour ago already. He looks really good in the suit, by the way!
dialogue.lit: Yes, before an hour already. He looks by-the-way really good out in-the suit!

dialogue.t: —Sieht sie nicht wunderschön aus, Tom? Du wirst gleich heiraten!
dialogue.en: Doesn't she look beautiful, Tom? You're about to get married!
dialogue.lit: Looks she not wonderfully-beautiful out, Tom? You become right-away marry!

dialogue.t: —Ich kann's selbst kaum fassen. Mein Herz schlägt so schnell gerade.
dialogue.en: I can hardly take it in myself. My heart's beating so fast right now.
dialogue.lit: I can-it myself barely grasp. My heart beats so fast right-now.

dialogue.t: —Ganz ruhig, das wird wunderschön. Sie kommt jetzt den Gang entlang.
dialogue.en: Stay calm, it's going to be beautiful. She's coming down the aisle now.
dialogue.lit: Quite calmly, that becomes wonderfully-beautiful. She comes now the aisle along.

dialogue.t: —Tom, willst du Sophie zu deiner Frau nehmen?
dialogue.en: Tom, do you take Sophie to be your wife?
dialogue.lit: Tom, want you Sophie to your wife take?

dialogue.t: —Ich will! Mit ganzem Herzen, für immer.
dialogue.en: I do! With my whole heart, forever.
dialogue.lit: I want! With whole heart, for ever.

dialogue.t: —Sophie, willst du Tom zu deinem Mann nehmen?
dialogue.en: Sophie, do you take Tom to be your husband?
dialogue.lit: Sophie, want you Tom to your husband take?

dialogue.t: —Ich will, von ganzem Herzen!
dialogue.en: I do, with all my heart!
dialogue.lit: I want, of whole heart!

dialogue.t: —*Sie tauschen die Ringe* — Ich erkläre euch jetzt zu Mann und Frau!
dialogue.en: *They exchange rings* — I now pronounce you husband and wife!
dialogue.lit: *They exchange the rings* — I declare you-all now to husband and wife!

dialogue.t: —*Alle weinen vor Freude und klatschen* — Endlich, Tom und Sophie!
dialogue.en: *Everyone cries with joy and claps* — Finally, Tom and Sophie!
dialogue.lit: *All cry before joy and clap* — Finally, Tom and Sophie!

dialogue.t: —Schaut mal, das Brautpaar bereitet sich auf den ersten Tanz vor.
dialogue.en: Look, the bride and groom are getting ready for the first dance.
dialogue.lit: Look once, the bride-couple prepares itself on the first dance before.

dialogue.t: —Wie schön! Die Band fängt gerade an zu spielen.
dialogue.en: How lovely! The band's just starting to play.
dialogue.lit: How beautiful! The band starts right-now on to-play.

dialogue.t: —*Die Musik spielt* — Sieh sie dir an, sie strahlen beide so.
dialogue.en: *The music plays* — Look at them, they're both glowing so much.
dialogue.lit: *The music plays* — Look them to-yourself, they shine both so.

dialogue.t: —Ich hab ein bisschen Tränen der Rührung, ehrlich gesagt.
dialogue.en: I have a few tears of emotion, to be honest.
dialogue.lit: I have a little tears of-the touched-feeling, honestly said.

dialogue.t: —Geht mir genauso! Lukas, hast du auch eine Träne im Auge?
dialogue.en: Same with me! Lukas, do you have a tear in your eye too?
dialogue.lit: Goes to-me just-so! Lukas, have you also a tear in-the eye?

dialogue.t: —Vielleicht ein bisschen, ja. Das ist einfach so ein schöner Moment.
dialogue.en: Maybe a little, yes. This is just such a beautiful moment.
dialogue.lit: Maybe a little, yes. That is simply so a beautiful moment.

dialogue.t: —Sollen wir auch tanzen gehen, wenn die Musik weitergeht?
dialogue.en: Should we go dance too, when the music continues?
dialogue.lit: Shall we also dance go, when the music further-goes?

dialogue.t: —Auf jeden Fall! Eine Hochzeit ohne Tanzen ist doch keine richtige Hochzeit.
dialogue.en: Definitely! A wedding without dancing isn't a real wedding after all.
dialogue.lit: On every case! A wedding without dancing is indeed no real wedding.

dialogue.t: —Lukas, du wolltest doch eine Rede halten, oder? Es ist gleich so weit.
dialogue.en: Lukas, you wanted to give a speech, right? It's almost time.
dialogue.lit: Lukas, you wanted indeed a speech to-hold, or? It is right-away so far.

dialogue.t: —Ja, ich bin schon ein bisschen nervös, ehrlich gesagt.
dialogue.en: Yes, I'm already a bit nervous, honestly.
dialogue.lit: Yes, I am already a little nervous, honestly said.

dialogue.t: —Du schaffst das! Heb einfach dein Glas und sprich von Herzen.
dialogue.en: You'll manage it! Just raise your glass and speak from the heart.
dialogue.lit: You manage that! Raise simply your glass and speak from heart.

dialogue.t: —Okay, dann mach ich das jetzt. *Er erhebt das Glas* — Tom und Sophie, ihr seid meine besten Freunde.
dialogue.en: Okay, I'll do it now then. *He raises the glass* — Tom and Sophie, you're my best friends.
dialogue.lit: Okay, then make I that now. *He raises the glass* — Tom and Sophie, you-all are my best friends.

dialogue.t: —Auch wenn ich weit weg wohne, seid ihr mir immer nah. Auf das Brautpaar!
dialogue.en: Even though I live far away, you're always close to me. To the bride and groom!
dialogue.lit: Even-though I far away live, are you-all to-me always near. To the bride-couple!

dialogue.t: —*Alle stoßen an, einige haben Tränen in den Augen* — Das war so rührend, Lukas!
dialogue.en: *Everyone toasts, some have tears in their eyes* — That was so touching, Lukas!
dialogue.lit: *All clink-on, some have tears in the eyes* — That was so touching, Lukas!

dialogue.t: —Danke. Jetzt bist du dran, Anna, oder?
dialogue.en: Thanks. Now it's your turn, Anna, right?
dialogue.lit: Thanks. Now are you on-turn, Anna, or?

dialogue.t: —Stimmt! Okay, ich heb auch mein Glas. Auf meine wundervolle Schwester!
dialogue.en: True! Okay, I'm raising my glass too. To my wonderful sister!
dialogue.lit: True! Okay, I raise also my glass. To my wonderful sister!

lessons.titleEn: Late at night at the wedding

dialogue.t: —Wow, wie spät ist es eigentlich? Ich bin barfuß und total müde.
dialogue.en: Wow, what time is it actually? I'm barefoot and totally tired.
dialogue.lit: Wow, how late is it actually? I am barefoot and totally tired.

dialogue.t: —Schon fast Mitternacht, glaub ich. Aber müde und glücklich, oder?
dialogue.en: Almost midnight already, I think. But tired and happy, right?
dialogue.lit: Already almost midnight, believe I. But tired and happy, or?

dialogue.t: —Total. Schau dir die Sterne an, so klar heut Nacht.
dialogue.en: Totally. Look at the stars, so clear tonight.
dialogue.lit: Totally. Look you-to-yourself the stars, so clear today night.

dialogue.t: —Schön, oder? Das war wirklich der schönste Tag, den ich je erlebt hab.
dialogue.en: Beautiful, isn't it? This was really the most beautiful day I've ever experienced.
dialogue.lit: Beautiful, or? That was really the most-beautiful day, that I ever experienced have.

dialogue.t: —Find ich auch. Komm, lass uns den letzten Song noch mittanzen.
dialogue.en: I think so too. Come on, let's dance to the last song too.
dialogue.lit: Find I also. Come, let us the last song still along-dance.

dialogue.t: —*Sie tanzen Hand in Hand* — Diese Nacht vergess ich mein Leben lang nicht.
dialogue.en: *They dance hand in hand* — I won't forget this night for the rest of my life.
dialogue.lit: *They dance hand in hand* — This night forget I my life long not.

dialogue.t: —Ich auch nicht. Wir alle zusammen, an diesem einen wunderschönen Tag.
dialogue.en: Me neither. All of us together, on this one beautiful day.
dialogue.lit: I also not. We all together, on this one wonderfully-beautiful day.

dialogue.t: —Genau. Lass uns das einfach noch ein bisschen genießen, bevor's vorbei ist.
dialogue.en: Exactly. Let's just enjoy this a little more before it's over.
dialogue.lit: Exactly. Let us that simply still a little enjoy, before-it over is.

dialogue.t: —Boah, was für eine Nacht! Mein Kopf brummt ein bisschen.
dialogue.en: Wow, what a night! My head's buzzing a little.
dialogue.lit: Whoa, what for a night! My head buzzes a little.

dialogue.t: —Haha, kein Wunder, wir haben ja bis spät getanzt und gefeiert.
dialogue.en: Haha, no wonder, we danced and celebrated until late after all.
dialogue.lit: Haha, no wonder, we have indeed until late danced and celebrated.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt. Aber war's nicht einfach die schönste Hochzeit überhaupt?
dialogue.en: True. But wasn't that simply the most beautiful wedding ever?
dialogue.lit: True. But was-it not simply the most-beautiful wedding at-all?

dialogue.t: —Total! Sophie und Tom sahen so glücklich aus, und Lukas' Rede war so rührend.
dialogue.en: Totally! Sophie and Tom looked so happy, and Lukas' speech was so touching.
dialogue.lit: Totally! Sophie and Tom looked so happy out, and Lukas' speech was so touching.

dialogue.t: —Genau, ich musste echt ein paar Tränen wegwischen, ehrlich gesagt.
dialogue.en: Exactly, I really had to wipe away a few tears, honestly.
dialogue.lit: Exactly, I had-to really a-few tears wipe-away, honestly said.

dialogue.t: —Geht mir genauso. Wann fährt eigentlich Lukas wieder zurück?
dialogue.en: Same with me. When does Lukas actually go back, by the way?
dialogue.lit: Goes to-me just-so. When drives actually Lukas again back?

dialogue.t: —Erst morgen, zum Glück. Wir haben noch einen ganzen Tag zusammen.
dialogue.en: Not until tomorrow, thankfully. We still have a whole day together.
dialogue.lit: First tomorrow, to-the luck. We have still a whole day together.

dialogue.t: —Perfekt. Lass uns den heute richtig nutzen, bevor er wieder weg ist.
dialogue.en: Perfect. Let's really make the most of today before he's gone again.
dialogue.lit: Perfect. Let us today properly use, before he again away is.

dialogue.t: —Genau. Frühstück zusammen, dann Park, wie immer halt.
dialogue.en: Exactly. Breakfast together, then the park, like always, basically.
dialogue.lit: Exactly. Breakfast together, then park, like always just-well.

dialogue.t: —Weißt du noch, wie wir uns alle das erste Mal kennengelernt haben?
dialogue.en: Do you remember how we all first met?
dialogue.lit: Know you still, how we us all the first time met have?

dialogue.t: —Klar! Auf dem Markt, mit Lukas und den frischen Äpfeln.
dialogue.en: Sure! At the market, with Lukas and the fresh apples.
dialogue.lit: Clear! On the market, with Lukas and the fresh apples.

dialogue.t: —Und dann kamen Sophie und Tom dazu, mit dieser ganzen Verwechslungsgeschichte.
dialogue.en: And then Sophie and Tom joined, with that whole mix-up story.
dialogue.lit: And then came Sophie and Tom to-it, with this whole mix-up-story.

dialogue.t: —Haha, stimmt! Ich hab Tom echt für Lukas gehalten, am Anfang.
dialogue.en: Haha, true! I really took Tom for Lukas, at the start.
dialogue.lit: Haha, true! I have Tom really for Lukas held, at-the start.

dialogue.t: —Und jetzt sind Sophie und Tom verheiratet, Anna hat ihren eigenen Laden.
dialogue.en: And now Sophie and Tom are married, Anna has her own shop.
dialogue.lit: And now are Sophie and Tom married, Anna has her own shop.

dialogue.t: —Und Lukas wohnt in einer ganz neuen Stadt, aber ist immer noch bei uns, irgendwie.
dialogue.en: And Lukas lives in a whole new city, but is still with us somehow.
dialogue.lit: And Lukas lives in a whole new city, but is still with us, somehow.

dialogue.t: —Wenn ich drüber nachdenke, war jede einzelne Erinnerung so wertvoll.
dialogue.en: When I think about it, every single memory was so valuable.
dialogue.lit: When I about-it after-think, was every single memory so valuable.

dialogue.t: —Total. Vom ersten Kaffee bis zur Hochzeit — was für eine Reise.
dialogue.en: Totally. From the first coffee to the wedding — what a journey.
dialogue.lit: Totally. From-the first coffee to-the wedding — what for a journey.

dialogue.t: —Und das Beste ist: Wir sind noch lange nicht fertig damit.
dialogue.en: And the best part is: we're far from done with it.
dialogue.lit: And the best is: we are still long not finished with-it.

dialogue.t: —Anna, du siehst so traurig aus. Ist alles okay?
dialogue.en: Anna, you look so sad. Is everything okay?
dialogue.lit: Anna, you look so sad out. Is everything okay?

dialogue.t: —Nicht wirklich, ehrlich gesagt. Meine Großmutter ist im Krankenhaus.
dialogue.en: Not really, to be honest. My grandmother's in the hospital.
dialogue.lit: Not really, honestly said. My grandmother is in-the hospital.

dialogue.t: —Oh nein, das tut mir so leid. Mach dir keine Sorgen, sie wird das schaffen.
dialogue.en: Oh no, I'm so sorry. Don't worry, she'll manage it.
dialogue.lit: Oh no, that does to-me so sorry. Make to-yourself no worries, she becomes that manage.

dialogue.t: —Ich hoffe es so sehr. Ich mach mir wirklich Sorgen, Sophie.
dialogue.en: I hope so very much. I'm really worried, Sophie.
dialogue.lit: I hope it so much. I make to-myself really worries, Sophie.

dialogue.t: —Das ist völlig verständlich. Komm her, lass dich umarmen.
dialogue.en: That's completely understandable. Come here, let me hug you.
dialogue.lit: That is completely understandable. Come here, let yourself embrace.

dialogue.t: —Danke, das tut gut. Ich weiß nicht, was ich ohne euch alle machen würde.
dialogue.en: Thanks, that feels good. I don't know what I'd do without all of you.
dialogue.lit: Thanks, that does good. I know not, what I without you-all make would.

dialogue.t: —Wir sind doch da für dich, immer. Wir halten zusammen, egal was passiert.
dialogue.en: We're here for you, always. We stick together, no matter what happens.
dialogue.lit: We are indeed there for you, always. We hold together, equal what happens.

dialogue.t: —Danke, Sophie. Ich fahr morgen früh ins Krankenhaus, willst du mitkommen?
dialogue.en: Thanks, Sophie. I'm going to the hospital tomorrow morning, want to come along?
dialogue.lit: Thanks, Sophie. I drive tomorrow early into-the hospital, want you along-come?

dialogue.t: —Natürlich komm ich mit. Du musst da nicht allein hin.
dialogue.en: Of course I'll come along. You don't have to go there alone.
dialogue.lit: Naturally come I along. You must there not alone to.

dialogue.t: —Oma, ich bin hier. Wie geht es dir heute?
dialogue.en: Grandma, I'm here. How are you feeling today?
dialogue.lit: Grandma, I am here. How goes it to-you today?

dialogue.t: —Schon viel besser, mein Schatz. Der Arzt sagt, es geht mir besser.
dialogue.en: Much better already, sweetheart. The doctor says I'm doing better.
dialogue.lit: Already much better, my treasure. The doctor says, it goes to-me better.

dialogue.t: —Das freut mich so sehr zu hören! Ich hab mir solche Sorgen gemacht.
dialogue.en: That makes me so happy to hear! I was so worried.
dialogue.lit: That pleases me so much to hear! I have to-myself such worries made.

dialogue.t: —Ich weiß, mein Schatz. Aber jetzt bin ich ja stark, und du bist hier.
dialogue.en: I know, sweetheart. But now I'm strong, and you're here.
dialogue.lit: I know, my treasure. But now am I indeed strong, and you are here.

dialogue.t: —*Sie hält die Hand ihrer Großmutter* — Ich bin so froh, dich zu sehen, Oma.
dialogue.en: *She holds her grandmother's hand* — I'm so glad to see you, Grandma.
dialogue.lit: *She holds the hand of-her grandmother* — I am so glad, you to see, Grandma.

dialogue.t: —Sophie, du bist auch hier? Wie nett von dir, vorbeizukommen.
dialogue.en: Sophie, you're here too? How nice of you to come by.
dialogue.lit: Sophie, you are also here? How nice of you, by-to-come.

dialogue.t: —Natürlich bin ich hier. Anna ist meine beste Freundin, klar komm ich mit.
dialogue.en: Of course I'm here. Anna's my best friend, of course I'd come along.
dialogue.lit: Naturally am I here. Anna is my best friend, clear come I along.

dialogue.t: —Ihr seid beide so lieb. Jetzt erzählt mal leise, was bei euch so los ist.
dialogue.en: You're both so sweet. Now tell me quietly what's going on with you.
dialogue.lit: You-all are both so dear. Now tell once quietly, what at you-all so loose is.

dialogue.t: —Anna, wie geht's deiner Großmutter mittlerweile? Schon besser?
dialogue.en: Anna, how's your grandmother doing now? Better already?
dialogue.lit: Anna, how goes-it to-your grandmother meanwhile? Already better?

dialogue.t: —Ja, viel besser, danke der Nachfrage. Sie darf bald nach Hause.
dialogue.en: Yes, much better, thanks for asking. She gets to go home soon.
dialogue.lit: Yes, much better, thanks of-the asking. She may soon to home.

dialogue.t: —Das ist so eine gute Nachricht! Ich war echt besorgt um euch beide.
dialogue.en: That's such good news! I was really worried about you both.
dialogue.lit: That is such a good news! I was really worried about you-all both.

dialogue.t: —Danke, das bedeutet mir viel. Es war eine schwere Zeit, ehrlich gesagt.
dialogue.en: Thanks, that means a lot to me. It was a hard time, honestly.
dialogue.lit: Thanks, that means to-me much. It was a hard time, honestly said.

dialogue.t: —Verständlich. Aber ihr habt zusammengehalten, das war wichtig.
dialogue.en: Understandable. But you stuck together, that was important.
dialogue.lit: Understandable. But you-all have together-held, that was important.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt total. Ohne euch alle wär's viel schwerer gewesen.
dialogue.en: Totally true. Without all of you it would have been much harder.
dialogue.lit: True totally. Without you-all all would-be-it much harder been.

dialogue.t: —Dafür sind wir doch da. In guten und in schlechten Zeiten.
dialogue.en: That's what we're here for, after all. In good times and bad.
dialogue.lit: For-that are we indeed there. In good and in bad times.

dialogue.t: —Genau das. Ich bin so dankbar für diese Freundschaft, wirklich.
dialogue.en: Exactly that. I'm so grateful for this friendship, really.
dialogue.lit: Exactly that. I am so grateful for this friendship, really.

dialogue.t: —Ich kann's kaum glauben, drei Jahre sind schon vergangen!
dialogue.en: I can hardly believe it, three years have already passed!
dialogue.lit: I can-it barely believe, three years are already passed!

dialogue.t: —Wahnsinn, oder? Und schaut euch alle an, wir kommen endlich wieder zusammen.
dialogue.en: Crazy, right? And look at everyone, we're finally getting together again.
dialogue.lit: Madness, or? And look you-to-yourselves all, we come finally again together.

dialogue.t: —Lukas, du hast dich kaum verändert! Genau wie damals.
dialogue.en: Lukas, you've barely changed! Just like back then.
dialogue.lit: Lukas, you have yourself barely changed! Exactly like back-then.

dialogue.t: —Haha, danke! Ihr auch nicht, ehrlich. Die alte Truppe, einfach wieder vereint.
dialogue.en: Haha, thanks! You guys too, honestly. The old crew, just reunited again.
dialogue.lit: Haha, thanks! You-all also not, honestly. The old crew, simply again united.

dialogue.t: —Und schaut mal, Sophie und Tom haben sogar ein Baby dabei!
dialogue.en: And look, Sophie and Tom even have a baby with them!
dialogue.lit: And look once, Sophie and Tom have even a baby with-it!

dialogue.t: —Stimmt! Die Zeit vergeht echt schnell, wenn man's bedenkt.
dialogue.en: True! Time really flies when you think about it.
dialogue.lit: True! The time passes really fast, when one-it considers.

dialogue.t: —Total. Und Annas Laden läuft immer noch super, hab ich gehört.
dialogue.en: Totally. And Anna's shop is still doing great, I heard.
dialogue.lit: Totally. And Anna's shop runs still super, have I heard.

dialogue.t: —Ja, besser denn je! Kommt, lasst uns alle zusammen einen Kaffee trinken, wie früher.
dialogue.en: Yes, better than ever! Come on, let's all have coffee together, like in the old days.
dialogue.lit: Yes, better than ever! Come, let us all together a coffee drink, like earlier.

dialogue.t: —Weißt du noch, wie wir uns am Markt kennengelernt haben, Lukas?
dialogue.en: Do you remember how we met at the market, Lukas?
dialogue.lit: Know you still, how we us at-the market met have, Lukas?

dialogue.t: —Klar, wie könnt ich das vergessen! Du wolltest unbedingt frische Äpfel.
dialogue.en: Sure, how could I forget that! You absolutely wanted fresh apples.
dialogue.lit: Clear, how could I that forget! You wanted absolutely fresh apples.

dialogue.t: —Haha, stimmt! Und dann diese ganze Verwechslung mit Tom, am Anfang.
dialogue.en: Haha, true! And then that whole mix-up with Tom, at the start.
dialogue.lit: Haha, true! And then this whole mix-up with Tom, at-the start.

dialogue.t: —Ach ja, ich hab euch beide echt verwechselt! Sehr peinlich, ehrlich.
dialogue.en: Oh yes, I really mixed you two up! Pretty embarrassing, honestly.
dialogue.lit: Oh yes, I have you-all both really confused! Very embarrassing, honestly.

dialogue.t: —Und dann der Umzug, Sophies Beförderung, die Hochzeit — was für eine Reise.
dialogue.en: And then the move, Sophie's promotion, the wedding — what a journey.
dialogue.lit: And then the move, Sophie's promotion, the wedding — what for a journey.

dialogue.t: —Und durch alles haben wir zusammengehalten, oder? Egal was kam.
dialogue.en: And through everything we stuck together, right? No matter what came.
dialogue.lit: And through everything have we together-held, or? Equal what came.

dialogue.t: —Genau das. Das ist es, was eine echte Freundschaft ausmacht, denk ich.
dialogue.en: Exactly that. I think that's what makes a real friendship.
dialogue.lit: Exactly that. That is it, what a real friendship makes-out, think I.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt total. Auf uns, auf die Erinnerungen, und auf alles, was noch kommt!
dialogue.en: Totally true. To us, to the memories, and to everything still to come!
dialogue.lit: True totally. To us, to the memories, and to everything, what still comes!

dialogue.t: —Wisst ihr was? Lasst uns nochmal eine Reise zusammen machen, alle fünf.
dialogue.en: You know what? Let's take a trip together again, all five of us.
dialogue.lit: Know you-all what? Let us again a trip together make, all five.

dialogue.t: —Großartige Idee! Wohin denn? Vielleicht zurück, wo alles angefangen hat?
dialogue.en: Great idea! Where to though? Maybe back to where it all started?
dialogue.lit: Great idea! Where-to then? Maybe back, where everything begun has?

dialogue.t: —Stimmt, genau das! Zum Markt, zum Fitnessstudio, zur alten Bank im Park.
dialogue.en: True, exactly that! To the market, the gym, the old bench in the park.
dialogue.lit: True, exactly that! To-the market, to-the gym, to-the old bench in the park.

dialogue.t: —Das wär so schön, ein bisschen zurückblicken auf die alten Zeiten.
dialogue.en: That would be so nice, looking back a bit on the old times.
dialogue.lit: That would-be so beautiful, a little back-look on the old times.

dialogue.t: —Genau, und gleichzeitig sehen, wie weit wir alle gekommen sind.
dialogue.en: Exactly, and at the same time see how far we've all come.
dialogue.lit: Exactly, and simultaneously see, how far we all come are.

dialogue.t: —Ich bin so dankbar für jeden Einzelnen von euch, das müsst ihr wissen.
dialogue.en: I'm so grateful for each and every one of you, you should know.
dialogue.lit: I am so grateful for every single-one of you-all, that must you-all know.

dialogue.t: —Wir auch, Lukas! Egal wie weit weg, ihr alle gehört einfach zu mir.
dialogue.en: Us too, Lukas! No matter how far away, you all simply belong with me.
dialogue.lit: We also, Lukas! Equal how far away, you-all all belong simply to me.

dialogue.t: —Dann ist es beschlossen. Packt eure Koffer, nächstes Wochenende geht's los!
dialogue.en: Then it's decided. Pack your bags, next weekend it begins!
dialogue.lit: Then is-it decided. Pack your suitcases, next weekend goes-it off!

dialogue.t: —Da ist er, der Markt, genau wie damals! Riecht sogar genauso.
dialogue.en: There it is, the market, just like back then! It even smells the same.
dialogue.lit: There is it, the market, exactly like back-then! Smells even just-so.

dialogue.t: —Und schau, die alten Stände sind immer noch da. Verrückt, oder?
dialogue.en: And look, the old stalls are still there. Crazy, right?
dialogue.lit: And look, the old stands are still there. Crazy, or?

dialogue.t: —Lass uns nochmal frische Äpfel kaufen, wie beim ersten Mal.
dialogue.en: Let's buy fresh apples again, like the first time.
dialogue.lit: Let us again fresh apples buy, like at-the first time.

dialogue.t: —Gute Idee! Und danach gehen wir zum Fitnessstudio, wo Lukas trainiert hat.
dialogue.en: Good idea! And after that we'll go to the gym, where Lukas used to train.
dialogue.lit: Good idea! And after-that go we to-the gym, where Lukas trained has.

dialogue.t: —Haha, ich trainier da heute noch, übrigens! Nur halt nicht mehr jeden Tag.
dialogue.en: Haha, I still train there today, by the way! Just not every day anymore.
dialogue.lit: Haha, I train there today still, by-the-way! Only just-well not more every day.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt, und dann die Bank im Park, wo wir uns immer getroffen haben.
dialogue.en: True, and then the bench in the park, where we always used to meet.
dialogue.lit: True, and then the bench in the park, where we us always met have.

dialogue.t: —Genau! Lasst uns dort den Sonnenuntergang anschauen, wie immer.
dialogue.en: Exactly! Let's watch the sunset there, like always.
dialogue.lit: Exactly! Let us there the sunset look-at, like always.

dialogue.t: —Es fühlt sich an, als wäre keine Zeit vergangen, irgendwie.
dialogue.en: It feels like no time has passed, somehow.
dialogue.lit: It feels itself on, as-if no time passed, somehow.

dialogue.t: —Und gleichzeitig ist so viel passiert. Beides stimmt einfach.
dialogue.en: And at the same time so much has happened. Both are simply true.
dialogue.lit: And simultaneously is so much happened. Both true simply.

dialogue.t: —Schaut, der Tisch, an dem wir uns das allererste Mal alle getroffen haben.
dialogue.en: Look, the table where we all met for the very first time.
dialogue.lit: Look once, the table, at which we us the very-first time all met have.

dialogue.t: —Genau der gleiche Tisch! Lasst uns hier sitzen, wie damals.
dialogue.en: The exact same table! Let's sit here, like back then.
dialogue.lit: Exactly the same table! Let us here sit, like back-then.

dialogue.t: —Wenn ich zurückdenke an damals, hätte ich nie gedacht, wo wir heute stehen.
dialogue.en: When I think back to back then, I never would have thought where we'd be today.
dialogue.lit: When I back-think on back-then, would-have I never thought, where we today stand.

dialogue.t: —Ich auch nicht. Diese Reise des Lebens war einfach unglaublich.
dialogue.en: Me neither. This journey of life was simply incredible.
dialogue.lit: I also not. This journey of-the life was simply unbelievable.

dialogue.t: —Und sie ist ja noch lange nicht vorbei, oder? Es kommt noch so viel.
dialogue.en: And it's far from over, right? There's still so much to come.
dialogue.lit: And it is indeed still long not over, or? It comes still so much.

dialogue.t: —Stimmt total. Aber egal, was kommt, dieser Moment hier bleibt für immer in Erinnerung.
dialogue.en: Totally true. But no matter what comes, this moment here stays in our memory forever.
dialogue.lit: True totally. But equal, what comes, this moment here stays for ever in memory.

dialogue.t: —Lasst uns einen letzten Toast machen, am alten Tisch, mit der alten Truppe.
dialogue.en: Let's make one last toast, at the old table, with the old crew.
dialogue.lit: Let us a last toast make, at the old table, with the old crew.

dialogue.t: —Auf uns! Auf alles, was war, und alles, was noch kommt!
dialogue.en: To us! To everything that was, and everything still to come!
dialogue.lit: To us! To everything, what was, and everything, what still comes!

dialogue.t: —Also, was kommt als Nächstes für uns alle, würdet ihr sagen?
dialogue.en: So, what comes next for all of us, would you say?
dialogue.lit: So, what comes as next for us all, would-you-all say?

dialogue.t: —Keine Ahnung, ehrlich. Aber ich freu mich auf jedes neue Kapitel.
dialogue.en: No idea, honestly. But I'm looking forward to every new chapter.
dialogue.lit: No idea, honestly. But I rejoice me on every new chapter.

dialogue.t: —Genau das! Das Leben geht ja immer weiter, mit neuen Geschichten.
dialogue.en: Exactly that! Life just keeps going, with new stories.
dialogue.lit: Exactly that! The life goes indeed always further, with new stories.

dialogue.t: —Und egal, was als Nächstes passiert — wir sind füreinander da. Immer.
dialogue.en: And no matter what happens next — we're there for each other. Always.
dialogue.lit: And equal, what as next happens — we are for-each-other there. Always.

dialogue.t: —Das steht fest, egal wie weit weg wir wohnen oder was wir machen.
dialogue.en: That's certain, no matter how far away we live or what we do.
dialogue.lit: That stands fixed, equal how far away we live or what we make.

dialogue.t: —Auf die Zukunft also! Und auf alles, was sie uns bringt.
dialogue.en: To the future, then! And to everything it brings us.
dialogue.lit: To the future thus! And to everything, that it to-us brings.

dialogue.t: —Auf die Zukunft! Und auf diese Freundschaft, die einfach für immer hält.
dialogue.en: To the future! And to this friendship, which simply lasts forever.
dialogue.lit: To the future! And to this friendship, that simply for ever holds.

dialogue.t: —Genau. Egal was kommt, wir schaffen das immer zusammen.
dialogue.en: Exactly. No matter what comes, we always manage it together.
dialogue.lit: Exactly. Equal what comes, we manage that always together.

dialogue.t: —Kannst du es glauben? Wir haben das alles zusammen geschafft, von Anfang bis Ende.
dialogue.en: Can you believe it? We made it through all of this together, from start to finish.
dialogue.lit: Can you it believe? We have that all together managed, from start to end.

dialogue.t: —Ehrlich gesagt, kaum. Von 'Hallo, wie geht's' bis hierher — was für ein Weg.
dialogue.en: Honestly, barely. From 'Hi, how's it going' to here — what a journey.
dialogue.lit: Honestly said, barely. From 'hi, how goes-it' to-here — what for a way.

dialogue.t: —Und du, der das hier liest, gehörst jetzt auch dazu, weißt du das?
dialogue.en: And you, reading this — you're part of this now too, you know that?
dialogue.lit: And you, who that here read, belong now also to-it, know you that?

dialogue.t: —Stimmt total! Du warst die ganze Zeit bei uns, am Markt, bei der Hochzeit, überall.
dialogue.en: Totally true! You were with us the whole time, at the market, at the wedding, everywhere.
dialogue.lit: True totally! You were the whole time with us, at the market, at the wedding, everywhere.

dialogue.t: —Und jetzt sprichst du Deutsch, einfach so, ohne es richtig zu merken.
dialogue.en: And now you speak German, just like that, without really noticing it.
dialogue.lit: And now speak you German, simply so, without it properly to notice.

dialogue.t: —Genau das war der Plan die ganze Zeit. Geschichten statt Listen, Freunde statt Vokabeln.
dialogue.en: That was the plan the whole time. Stories instead of lists, friends instead of vocabulary.
dialogue.lit: Exactly that was the plan the whole time. Stories instead-of lists, friends instead-of vocabulary.

dialogue.t: —Wir sind so stolz auf dich. Du hast hundert Lektionen geschafft, ehrlich beeindruckend.
dialogue.en: We're so proud of you. You made it through a hundred lessons, honestly impressive.
dialogue.lit: We are so proud on you. You have hundred lessons managed, honestly impressive.

dialogue.t: —Aber das hier ist kein Abschied, klar? Du kannst jederzeit zu uns zurückkommen.
dialogue.en: But this isn't goodbye, okay? You can come back to us anytime.
dialogue.lit: But this here is no goodbye, clear? You can at-any-time to us back-come.

dialogue.t: —Genau. Max, Anna, Lukas, Sophie, Tom — wir bleiben hier, für immer deine Familie.
dialogue.en: Exactly. Max, Anna, Lukas, Sophie, Tom — we'll stay here, forever your family.
dialogue.lit: Exactly. Max, Anna, Lukas, Sophie, Tom — we stay here, for ever your family.

dialogue.t: —Auf dich! Auf hundert Lektionen, und auf alles, was du als Nächstes lernst. Mach's gut!
dialogue.en: To you! To a hundred lessons, and to everything you learn next. Take care!
dialogue.lit: To you! To hundred lessons, and to everything, what you as next learn. Make-it good!
